name: Salesforce MCP Server (Cirra AI)
what_it_does: Community MCP server for Salesforce CRM enabling AI agents to query Salesforce objects via SOQL, manage records (leads, contacts, opportunities), and interact with Salesforce data in agent sales workflows.
use_cases: Querying Salesforce objects (leads, contacts, opportunities) from agents
Creating and updating CRM records in agent-driven sales workflows
Running SOQL queries for pipeline analysis and reporting
Integrating Salesforce data into AI sales assistants
Automating Salesforce record updates from agent workflows
not_for: Salesforce Marketing Cloud or Commerce Cloud (separate products)
Real-time streaming (Salesforce is request/response, not streaming)
best_when: An agent needs to read or update Salesforce CRM data — pipeline status, account records, contact management — as part of a sales or RevOps workflow.
avoid_when: You don't use Salesforce CRM, or need real-time event streaming from Salesforce (use Salesforce Platform Events for that).

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 via Connected App (recommended). Username + password flow available but deprecated for production. OAuth scopes control API access. JWT Bearer for server-to-server is most agent-appropriate.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer Org: free forever with API access
pricing.notes: Free Developer Org available for testing. Production Salesforce is expensive. API access included in most plans. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: Daily API limits: 15,000-100,000+ calls/day depending on edition

known_agent_gotchas: SOQL has a 50,000 record governor limit per query — paginate large result sets
OAuth Connected App setup is complex (15+ configuration steps in Salesforce admin UI)
Daily API limits are org-wide — a misbehaving agent can exhaust limits for all users
Object API names vs labels differ (e.g., Opportunity vs OpportunityLineItem) — use API names
Community MCP — not official Salesforce; Salesforce has their own Agentforce platform now
Sandbox vs production orgs have different URLs and credentials — easy to mix up
agent_readiness.security_notes: Salesforce has excellent security but setup is complex. JWT Bearer OAuth is the recommended server-to-server pattern. Profile/permission set controls limit what agents can see and do. HTTPS enforced.

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth with profile/permission set restrictions provides strong access control. Complex but secure when configured correctly. Enterprise compliance certifications.
